label: BREAKING
hook: CENTCOM REQUESTS HYPERSONIC MISSILES FOR IRAN
stat2: US has 1 operational hypersonic: Dark Eagle
stat3: Last US strike on Iran: Soleimani, Jan 2020
tts_text: Breaking. U.S. Central Command requests hypersonic missile deployment in the Middle East, for possible use against Iran. A request the U.S. has only made once before, and the most aggressive military signal yet on Iran.
caption:
US Central Command (CENTCOM) just requested deployment of hypersonic missiles to the Middle East for possible use against Iran.

What's not in the headline:

CENTCOM requests aren't deployments. Deployments aren't strikes. The chain from "requested" to "used" usually involves White House sign-off, congressional notification, and weeks-to-months of pre-positioning.

The US has exactly one operational hypersonic weapon: the Long Range Hypersonic Weapon (Dark Eagle), with limited stockpiles. Pre-positioning a handful of these is a signal, not a strike.

Last time the US directly killed an Iranian senior official: Qasem Soleimani in January 2020. The escalation pattern that followed was contained through back-channel diplomacy, not military force.

Read this as posture, not action. The story is who's leaking the request and why.

— The Clam Letter
